pattern: Walking the multiples of k in order and checking presence in a set finds the first gap without scanning the array repeatedly.
hints: You only care about values that are multiples of k, in increasing order: k, 2k, 3k, ...
Put the array values in a set so each presence check is constant time.
Start at m = k and keep adding k until m is not in the set, then return m.

edge_inputs.description: first few multiples present, gap further out
k of one returns the smallest absent positive
array has non-multiples that must be ignored
large k whose first multiple exceeds all values
consecutive multiples present so answer is the next one

stress.description: random small arrays and k so multiples are densely or sparsely present
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, rng.randrange(1, size + 1))
    nums = [rng.randrange(1, 101) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randrange(1, 101)
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: values capped at 100 so the missing multiple is found quickly
